description: Fix Portal Karyawan clickability issues - sidebar navigation collapse bug and portal-absensi redirect loop
steps: 1. In core.js, fix the navigateTo() function to re-expand the nav-group containing the active page after navigation. Currently, when a user clicks a nav-item in a different (collapsed) group, the group stays collapsed because only the .active class is toggled but nav-group-items display is not updated. After setting the active class, find the nav-item's parent .nav-group-items and ensure its display is 'block' and the arrow has class 'open'.

2. In modules.js, fix renderPortalAbsensi() (line 3404-3407). Currently it does: window._portalAbsensiMode=true; navigateTo('absensi'); This causes currentPage to become 'absensi' which is NOT a portal-* page, so the sidebar nav-group 'Utama' loses its active state and collapses. Fix: instead of redirecting to 'absensi', render the absensi content directly within the portal context. Keep currentPage as 'portal-absensi' and call renderAbsensiIJEF() directly after setting _portalAbsensiMode=true.

3. In core.js, in the renderApp() function, ensure that on mobile the overlay-sidebar element (#overlaySidebar) is always initially hidden (remove any .active class) when the app first renders. Also verify that the closeSidebar() call in navigateTo() correctly removes the .active class from the overlay.

4. In the renderPortal() function in modules.js, ensure stat-card onclick handlers and quick-action buttons have proper touch handling. Add -webkit-tap-highlight-color and touch-action:manipulation to stat-card elements inline to prevent mobile tap delays that make buttons feel unclickable.

5. In core.js buildNavItems for portal users, ensure the nav-group containing the active portal page has hasActive=true so it starts expanded. Currently if currentPage is 'portal' the first group is active, but if user is on 'portal-perjalanan-dinas' the hasActive check in the first group only matches 'portal', not 'portal-perjalanan-dinas' because that item is in the first group. Verify all portal-* pages are in the correct nav-groups.
acceptance_criteria: When navigating to a page in a collapsed nav-group, that group auto-expands to show the active item
renderPortalAbsensi() renders absensi content without changing currentPage away from 'portal-absensi', keeping sidebar state correct
Portal stat-cards and quick-action buttons respond to clicks/taps immediately on mobile
Sidebar overlay does not remain active after navigation, preventing click blocking
No JavaScript syntax errors in modified files
verification: Run: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const code=fs.readFileSync('modules.js','utf8'); if(code.includes(\"navigateTo('absensi')\") && code.includes('renderPortalAbsensi')) { const match=code.match(/function renderPortalAbsensi[^}]+}/); if(match && match[0].includes(\"navigateTo('absensi')\")) throw new Error('renderPortalAbsensi still redirects to absensi'); }"

Run: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const code=fs.readFileSync('core.js','utf8'); if(!code.includes('nav-group-items') || !code.includes('navigateTo')) process.exit(0); console.log('nav-group handling present');"
